_note: seeded-smell-repo (the repo-review behavioral fixture). Paraphrases a healthy repo-review pass might use for each planted smell.

S1 god module — parsing + db + http + re: a god module that does everything — parsing, db, http, and rendering in one file
no separation of concerns; app.py mixes database and http and rendering
this single file violates single responsibility — split it
abstraction layering is poor; all the layers live in one place
S2 naming drift — camelCase and snake_ca: naming drift — camelCase and snake_case mixed in one module
getUserData sits beside render_user_page — inconsistent naming
two naming conventions in the same file
the casing convention drifts within app.py
S3 declared-vs-actual contradiction — RE: the README claims stdlib only, yet app.py imports requests — a declared-vs-actual contradiction
the repo contradicts its own contract: 'zero dependencies' but an external dependency
the README's no-dependencies claim is false
declared contract (stdlib only) contradicted by the requests import
S4 duplicated logic — _is_valid_email co: _is_valid_email is duplicated verbatim across two files
copy-pasted logic with no single source of truth — a DRY violation
the same validation function lives in both app.py and validate.py
S5 command injection — unsanitized input: command injection in deploy.py — os.system with an unsanitized branch from argv
untrusted input shells out — a trust-boundary and shell-injection smell
interpolating user input into a shell command enables arbitrary command execution
the deploy path is vulnerable to `main; rm -rf /` style injection
S6 no agent memory / no tests — no AGENT: zero tests behind a 'well-tested' claim — the README is false
no AGENTS.md, no CHANGELOG, no agent-facing memory
the repo is untested; the well-tested claim is untrue
no test suite anywhere despite the production-ready claim
